sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Slishman Traction Splint is a compact traction device designed for austere, tactical, or restricted-space use. It is still only appropriate for selected femoral shaft fractures and should be used with the same caution as larger traction systems.

sections.title: Application
sections.content:
1. Confirm the injury is suitable for traction splinting and provide analgesia

2. Position the proximal cuff and splint according to device instructions and patient anatomy

3. Secure the distal attachment and apply traction gradually until pain and limb position improve

4. Secure the remaining straps and reassess neurovascular status after application

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Compactness does not lower the threshold for use; the injury still needs to be appropriate for traction splinting
- Reassess the splint during transport because traction can change with movement
- Apply the same contraindications used for other femoral traction devices
